Workflow.Name: Boty - Anexa arquivo V2
Workflow.Description: When an agent calls the flow and send back a response.

Description.description: When an agent calls the flow and send back a response.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Identifica usuario
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when an email address is provided. It authenticates with an external system, retrieves a token, and uses it to search for a user matching the email. The flow then extracts the user's full name and unique code from the search results. Finally, it responds with the user's full name and code. The process is sequential, ensuring each step completes successfully before moving to the next, and uses loops to handle multiple user records if needed.

Description.description: This flow starts when an email address is provided. It authenticates with an external system, retrieves a token, and uses it to search for a user matching the email. The flow then extracts the user's full name and unique code from the search results. Finally, it responds with the user's full name and code. The process is sequential, ensuring each step completes successfully before moving to the next, and uses loops to handle multiple user records if needed.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Gera Token DeskManager
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when manually triggered. It sends a POST request to an external authentication API to obtain a token. The response is then parsed to extract the authentication token. Finally, the flow returns the token as a response to the requester. Each step depends on the successful completion of the previous one, ensuring the process is sequential and the token is securely retrieved and delivered.

Description.description: This flow starts when manually triggered. It sends a POST request to an external authentication API to obtain a token. The response is then parsed to extract the authentication token. Finally, the flow returns the token as a response to the requester. Each step depends on the successful completion of the previous one, ensuring the process is sequential and the token is securely retrieved and delivered.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Consulta chamados
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when it receives a request containing a token and an email. It uses these details to search for support tickets related to the email address. The flow retrieves and parses the five most recent tickets, then gathers the latest interaction for each one. It compiles key information—such as ticket number, creation date, status, responsible person, and the last message—into a formatted summary. Finally, it sends this summary back as a response, providing a quick overview of the user's recent support activity.

Description.description: This flow starts when it receives a request containing a token and an email. It uses these details to search for support tickets related to the email address. The flow retrieves and parses the five most recent tickets, then gathers the latest interaction for each one. It compiles key information—such as ticket number, creation date, status, responsible person, and the last message—into a formatted summary. Finally, it sends this summary back as a response, providing a quick overview of the user's recent support activity.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Interage no chamado
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when it receives a request with three pieces of information: a token, a ticket code, and a message. It processes these inputs, then sends an update to a support ticket system using the provided details. After the update, it creates a confirmation message indicating that a new interaction was made on the ticket. Finally, it sends this confirmation back as a response to the original request. The flow ensures each step is completed successfully before moving to the next.

Description.description: This flow starts when it receives a request with three pieces of information: a token, a ticket code, and a message. It processes these inputs, then sends an update to a support ticket system using the provided details. After the update, it creates a confirmation message indicating that a new interaction was made on the ticket. Finally, it sends this confirmation back as a response to the original request. The flow ensures each step is completed successfully before moving to the next.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Classifica chamado
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when it receives a request with two text inputs. It uses one input as a token and the other as a search term to look up categories from an external API. Based on the number of categories found, it sets the status and response: if none are found, it returns a default message; if one is found, it returns the exact match; if multiple are found, it loops through them and returns a list. The flow then responds with the appropriate category, subject, status, and, if needed, a detailed list.

Description.description: This flow starts when it receives a request with two text inputs. It uses one input as a token and the other as a search term to look up categories from an external API. Based on the number of categories found, it sets the status and response: if none are found, it returns a default message; if one is found, it returns the exact match; if multiple are found, it loops through them and returns a list. The flow then responds with the appropriate category, subject, status, and, if needed, a detailed list.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Consulta chamados por solicitante
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when it receives a manual trigger with user input. It stores search criteria, then sends a POST request to an external API to retrieve ticket data. The response is parsed, and for each ticket, details like ticket number, creation date, status, and responsible operator are formatted into a summary string. After processing all tickets, the flow returns the compiled summary as a response to the requester. The flow ensures each step only runs after the previous one succeeds, maintaining a logical sequence for data handling and output.

Description.description: This flow starts when it receives a manual trigger with user input. It stores search criteria, then sends a POST request to an external API to retrieve ticket data. The response is parsed, and for each ticket, details like ticket number, creation date, status, and responsible operator are formatted into a summary string. After processing all tickets, the flow returns the compiled summary as a response to the requester. The flow ensures each step only runs after the previous one succeeds, maintaining a logical sequence for data handling and output.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Abre Chamado
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when a manual trigger receives user input with four required fields. It then processes and stores these inputs step-by-step, preparing data for an API call to create a support ticket. After the ticket is created, the flow organizes the response, extracts the ticket reference, and sends it back to the requester as confirmation. Each step depends on the successful completion of the previous one, ensuring a logical and reliable sequence for ticket creation and response delivery.

Description.description: This flow starts when a manual trigger receives user input with four required fields. It then processes and stores these inputs step-by-step, preparing data for an API call to create a support ticket. After the ticket is created, the flow organizes the response, extracts the ticket reference, and sends it back to the requester as confirmation. Each step depends on the successful completion of the previous one, ensuring a logical and reliable sequence for ticket creation and response delivery.

Workflow.Name: Boty - Cria solicitante
Workflow.Description: This flow starts when it receives a request containing a token, first name, last name, and email. It then sends this information to an external user management API to create or update a user. After receiving a response, it processes the returned user ID and sends it back to the requester as confirmation. The flow ensures each step only runs after the previous one succeeds, maintaining a clear and reliable sequence for user creation and response.

Description.description: This flow starts when it receives a request containing a token, first name, last name, and email. It then sends this information to an external user management API to create or update a user. After receiving a response, it processes the returned user ID and sends it back to the requester as confirmation. The flow ensures each step only runs after the previous one succeeds, maintaining a clear and reliable sequence for user creation and response.
